mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.12% is near zero, compressing risk-free discount rates and making equities more attractive relative to bonds, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if the rank IC over any 2-year walk-forward window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% (currently 10%) signals substantial economic slack, prompting aggressive monetary and fiscal stimulus that eventually supports equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8%, or if the average excess return over UNRATE<=8% periods is not positive at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) jointly confirm robust consumer demand and labor market health, driving equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from RSAFS and PAYEMS (e.g., both above their 3-month moving average) does not produce a positive average 20-day forward return that is statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during the early stages of recovery from a recession signals strengthening economic momentum, which is particularly bullish for equities. However, late in the cycle the same signal can turn negative due to overheating fears.
failure_condition: If during identified early-cycle periods (first 12 months after NBER trough) the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is negative on average, or if the regime-conditional signal fails to outperform the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the market prices in prolonged accommodative policy, which provides a persistent tailwind for equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day S&P 500 return during FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods is not positive, or if the win rate is below 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic growth that sustains corporate earnings and boosts long-run equity returns over a quarterly (60-day) horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0 AND RSAFS growth > 0 (6-month MA basis)
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of months when both series are above their respective 6-month moving averages, or if the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the broadest measure of economic activity; a rebound from recession levels (current value 12973.0) signals the economy is healing, which is positive for equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day S&P 500 forward return is negative on average during periods of positive GDP growth (q/q), or if the information coefficient (IC) between GDP growth and 60-day returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates weak demand, which in a post-recession environment leads to continued central bank accommodation, supporting equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day S&P 500 return during low-inflation periods (CPI YoY < 2%) is not significantly positive (p>0.05 in a one-sided t-test), or if the signal's predictive rank IC is below 0.03 over 3 years.
